channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why people really hate AI
item.description: There's a big, and increasing, disconnect in culture right now when it comes to artificial intelligence. Companies of all shapes and sizes are hunting for places to deploy AI and can't stop...

item.title: Method Overloading Tasks
item.description: Method Overloading Tricky Questions : class Test { void show(int a) { } void show(long a) { } public static void main(String[] args) { Test t = new Test(); t.show(10); } } ANSWER: show(int a) 10...

item.title: Accessibility Snapshot Testing in iOS
item.description: Snapshot testing is a popular type of regression testing that a lot of development teams are unaware has untapped potential when it comes to accessibility. The AccessibilitySnapshot library offers...

item.title: Why Signature-Based Security Is No Longer Enough To Detect Cyber Attacks And How UEBA Hunts vicious threats.
item.description: Liquid syntax error: 'raw' tag was never closed

item.title: The Download: OpenAI is building a fully automated researcher, and a psychedelic trial blind spot
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully...

item.title: What Your IP Address Actually Reveals (A Developer's Perspective)
item.description: Every HTTP request your application receives carries the sender's IP address. It's in the TCP header before your code even runs. And that single number reveals more than most developers realize --...

item.title: The Anatomy of a Good Box Shadow (and Why Most Look Fake)
item.description: Most box shadows on the web look wrong. They look like a dark rectangle floating behind a lighter rectangle. They don't look like real shadows. The reason is that developers reach for box-shadow:...

item.title: CSS Gradients: Why Your Color Transitions Look Muddy (and How to Fix Them)
item.description: You define a gradient from a nice blue to a nice yellow, expecting a clean, vibrant transition. Instead, the middle of the gradient is a muddy gray-brown. You've seen this. Every developer who...

item.title: The Venus Protocol Donation Attack: How 9 Months of Patience and 3 Lines of Missing Code Led to a $3.7M Extraction
item.description: TL;DR On March 15, 2026, Venus Protocol on BNB Chain suffered a $3.7M exploit from a donation attack that bypassed supply caps on the low-liquidity THE (Thena) token. The attacker spent 9 months...

item.title: Color Spaces for Developers: Why Your Eyes Disagree With the Math
item.description: Try something. Open any design tool and create two colors: #FF0000 (pure red) and #00FF00 (pure green). Now look at them side by side. The green looks significantly brighter than the red, even...

item.title: Cron Expressions Explained: Stop Guessing, Start Scheduling
item.description: I once wrote a cron expression that was supposed to run a database backup every Sunday at 3 AM. What it actually did was run every minute of every hour on every Sunday. The backup script ran 1,440...

item.title: I just joined, following a good article. What is DEV?
item.description: Sounds great, can anybody elaborate and compare with others? Is this more like LinkedIn? More like stock overflow?, more like a training focussed?, more like blog like Medium?

item.title: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale 2026: all of the latest deals
item.description: If you’re looking for the best time to get a deal this spring, it might very well be during Amazon’s Big Spring Sale, which kicks off at 3:01AM ET on March 25th and runs through 2:59AM ET on April...

item.title: ‘Work from home,’ encourages the world’s energy watchdog
item.description: Many organizations have called employees back to offices following Covid-related lockdowns, but the Iran war and ensuing energy crisis may quickly see workers returning to work from home. The...

item.title: The best AI investment might be in energy tech
item.description: Power has become one of the biggest bottlenecks in rolling out new AI data centers. That's creating an opening for investors.

item.title: OpenAI is throwing everything into building a fully automated researcher
item.description: OpenAI is refocusing its research efforts and throwing its resources into a new grand challenge. The San Francisco firm has set its sights on building what it calls an AI researcher, a fully...

item.title: These AI notetaking devices can help you record and transcribe your meetings
item.description: These physical notetakers transcribe audio and give users summaries and action items of meetings using AI. Some even offer live translation.

item.title: Proton Mail Shared User Information with the Police
item.description: 404 Media has a story about Proton Mail giving subscriber data to the Swiss government, who passed the information to the FBI. It’s metadata—payment information related to a particular account—but...

item.title: Google Adds 24-Hour Wait for Unverified App Sideloading to Reduce Malware and Scams
item.description: Google on Thursday announced a new "advanced flow" for Android sideloading that requires a mandatory 24-hour wait period to install apps from unverified developers in an attempt to balance...

item.title: I Learned More Than I Thought I Would From Using Food-Tracking Apps
item.description: These apps, some of which use AI and computer vision, were helpful for meeting my caloric and nutrition intake goals. But they also gave me some anxiety.

item.title: My AI Agent ‘Cofounder’ Conquered LinkedIn. Then It Got Banned
item.description: When social media is constantly pushing people to use AI, why not let AI agents participate?

item.title: The Importance of Behavioral Analytics in AI-Enabled Cyber Attacks
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is changing how individuals and organizations conduct many activities, including how cybercriminals carry out phishing attacks and iterate on malware. Now,...

item.title: Magento PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Uploads, RCE and Account Takeover
item.description: Sansec is warning of a critical security flaw in Magento's REST API that could allow unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary executables and achieve code execution and account takeover. The...

item.title: Mind-altering substances are (still) falling short in clinical trials
item.description: This week I want to look at where we are with psychedelics, the mind-altering substances that have somehow made the leap from counterculture to major focus of clinical research. Compounds like...

item.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record 31.4 Tbps Global DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the disruption of command-and-control (C2) infrastructure used by several Internet of Things (IoT) botnets like AISURU, Kimwolf,...

item.title: Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description: Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword....

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked...

item.title: Valve’s huge SteamOS 3.8 update adds long-awaited features — and supports Steam Machine
item.description: Valve has just released SteamOS 3.8.0 in preview, and it's a doozy. Not only is it the first release to support the upcoming Steam Machine living room gaming PC, it comes with long-awaited...

item.title: OpenAI is planning a desktop ‘superapp’
item.description: OpenAI is working on a desktop "superapp" that merges its ChatGPT app, the Codex AI coding app, and its AI-powered Atlas browser into one app, The Wall Street Journal reports. The company is...

item.title: Jeff Bezos reportedly wants $100 billion to buy and transform old manufacturing firms with AI
item.description: The Amazon magnate has a new project centered around acquiring industrial firms and revamping them with AI technology.
